page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-32GB is a compute-oriented virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance, and it does not include GPU acceleration. The underlying x86_64 architecture supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates average multi-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling within the middle 50% of tested systems. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core results ranking in the bottom 10%. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, large-block uncached read and write bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly, reaching the top 25% of benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this server as a budget-friendly option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The lack of bundled storage means storage resources must be provisioned and factored in separately.

This server is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and basic database operations. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or memory-intensive workloads due to its weak single-core performance and lower uncached memory bandwidth.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with no bundled local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, highlighted by average Geekbench and PassMark CPU scores, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, single-core processing is weak, placing in the bottom 10% of benchmarks, and uncached memory bandwidth is limited. Qualitatively, the shared CPU model offers a cost-effective entry point for parallelizable workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and no bundled local storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-32GB is a KVM virtual server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server featuring sixteen AMD EPYC vCPUs and thirty-two gigabytes of RAM for parallelized, budget-conscious workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
No bundled local storage or public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded tasks
